behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure appears at a module handoff (collectors -> aggregation -> summary input), not inside one pure function. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: a Unit test for the payload-builder function can pass with a handcrafted full input object while production wiring silently omits deployment-log data before calling summary generation. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real OS/network/subprocess are not required to reproduce this specific omission; in-process fakes for all three source clients are enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: this behavior checks pre-summary payload composition, not model judgment and not multi-agent sequencing. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text only requires inclusion of source data in the handoff payload, not real network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the report orchestrator with three in-process fake source adapters returning distinct markers, run until the summary-generation call boundary, and assert the passed content contains markers from metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log in the expected structure.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is in cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter failure -> orchestrator -> section output), not just local string logic. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: a Unit test for the formatter can pass for a missing-section input, while real orchestration still aborts because an adapter exception is not caught. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process adapter fakes that raise timeout/HTTP errors can expose this behavior; real network is not strictly required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: correctness does not depend on model output quality and does not chain agentic decisions. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about graceful degradation on source failure, which can be induced deterministically with local stubs at module boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Run the orchestration path with two healthy fake sources and one fake source that raises an availability error; assert report generation completes, includes a `data unavailable` note for that section, and still includes data-backed sections.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this behavior is output-quality correctness of narrative generation (faithfulness to key values), which depends on real model decisions; deterministic stubs cannot validate this. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Integration test with a fixed mocked summary response always passes, while the real model can hallucinate or misstate values from the same input. MOCK-HIDE: mocking the model hides the exact failure mode being tested. MULTI-STEP: behavior states a single summary-generation step, not a multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because the behavior text names one summary step and does not require sequential model decisions across stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute summary generation against the real model with representative input datasets, then evaluate factual consistency by checking that key numeric/status values in the narrative match source inputs; run repeated trials to detect non-deterministic misrepresentation rates.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this is an end-to-end delivery claim requiring real HTTP/service calls and real email transport/inbox observation; in-process fakes can miss provider/auth/protocol failures. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Integration test with mocked SMTP/API and fake inbox passes, while production fails due to real provider TLS/auth policy and message never lands in the recipient inbox. MOCK-HIDE: mocked email dependencies can hide real delivery and routing failures. LLM-DEP: this behavior asserts successful end delivery, not model output quality; model judgment is not the correctness target here. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic rejected because behavior text is specifically about inbox delivery correctness, not whether generated language content is semantically correct.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled reporting pipeline against real data-source endpoints (or production-like reachable services) and real email delivery infrastructure to a test recipient mailbox; assert one complete report is received with expected subject/body structure and recipient targeting.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: failure handling spans real email-service outage semantics plus durable local filesystem persistence across scheduled executions (often separate process runs). ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: an Integration test with in-memory filesystem and mocked scheduler passes, while production fails because the first run writes to a non-persistent/permission-restricted path and the next real scheduled run cannot reload/retry. MOCK-HIDE: mocking network and filesystem can hide restart/persistence and path/permission issues. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: this behavior is delivery-fallback orchestration, not model-quality evaluation and not a multi-agent decision chain. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because no sequential agentic model decisions are required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Induce real email-service unreachability in a system test environment, execute one scheduled run to generate and persist the report to disk, then trigger the next scheduled run in a fresh process context and assert retry reads the saved artifact and delivers successfully when service recovers.
